title: Webinar vs Challenge: Choosing Your Launch Vehicle Quiz

questions.question: According to the decision matrix, which launch format has a slight edge for offers priced at $997-$2,997?

options.text: Webinar, because a single session creates enough urgency to justify the price
Challenge, because the multi-day format provides more touchpoints and trust-building
Neither -- this price range requires an application funnel exclusively
A hybrid model where you run both simultaneously

questions.explanation: At the $997-$2,997 price range, the challenge format has a slight edge because higher price points require more trust-building, and the multi-day format provides additional touchpoints to establish that trust.

questions.question: Why does the challenge model create the 'endowment effect' in participants?

options.text: Because challenges are always free, so participants feel they deserve something in return
Because the daily homework forces participants to buy before Day 5
Because participants invest time, energy, and effort over multiple days, making them reluctant to walk away from the momentum they built
Because challenge creators offer bigger discounts than webinar creators

questions.explanation: The endowment effect occurs because by Day 3 or 4, participants have invested real time and energy, gotten small wins, and built community relationships. Walking away from the paid offer feels like abandoning the momentum they already created.

questions.question: What is the recommended 'launch ladder' progression for creator-founders?

options.text: Start with a challenge, graduate to a webinar, then add evergreen
Start with an application funnel, then add a webinar, then a challenge
Start with a webinar, graduate to a challenge once the offer is validated, then add an application layer for high-ticket
Start with evergreen content, then run a challenge, then do live webinars

questions.explanation: The launch ladder starts with webinars (faster setup, lower risk, immediate feedback), graduates to challenges once the offer is validated with 2-3 successful webinar launches, and then adds an application layer when moving upmarket to higher-ticket offers.

questions.question: Using the five dimensions of the decision matrix (price point, audience temperature, transformation complexity, your energy/capacity, and community fit), evaluate your current or planned offer. Which format -- webinar or challenge -- is the better fit, and why? Be specific about how you scored on each dimension.
questions.aiPrompt: The student should evaluate their offer across all five dimensions: price point, audience temperature, transformation complexity, energy capacity, and community fit. Look for specific reasoning for each dimension and a clear conclusion about which format they chose. Evaluate whether their reasoning is sound given the frameworks from the lesson.
